replies.author: @H-321
replies.text:
⁠​⁠@tallaster-g7s dude I’ve already seen a comment like yours from more people where they say the same thing every time yet they don’t understand whatsoever. Disney is like a three to five headed dragon where by removing one will do nothing because another suit will just replace it 

Kathleen is only one piece of the whole puzzle, with the others being Bob, Feige, Dave and the bankers who are above Disney 

Removing Kathleen isn’t gonna do anything because if that’s the case then by that logic why didn’t Bob or the people above her not remove her from Star Wars knowing the stuff that she was doing wasn’t good? You people think it’s just a Kathleen problem when it’s more than her and that’s the thing you don’t understand here

And I’m not saying she doesn’t deserve any of the flack because she definitely does but it’s more than just her who’s at fault here and that’s the point you aren’t understanding. This goes beyond her

replies.author: @tallaster-g7s
replies.text: @H-321 No this does NOT go beyond her. I am holding her responsible for the decisions she made. You are trying to make excuses  on why she is NOT responsible for the decisions that she made. That dog don't bark. This does NOT go beyond her. This is ALL on her.

replies.author: @H-321
replies.text:
@tallaster-g7s dude KATHLEEN is the ceo of STAR WARS, which is under the Disney umbrella and who owns that? It’s Bob, Kathleen is only part of the problem, stop being delusional by thinking she’s the only one at fault here. I used to have the same mindset as you with that and believed she was the problem but that was when I understood and realised that there are others above her who are also part of the problem. It’s not just her and you need to get out of that mindset

Read my comment again because where in that does it say I am defending her when I’m not, I don’t even like that woman, I don’t even like Disney as a whole but I’m not so delusional to think that she’s the only person at fault here like what you are saying rn

replies.author: @bloodlinefilms
replies.text: @spear1504canon was always downstream from the movies. if george decided to do something in the prequels that directly contradicted a book or something guess what just became the canon instead? the problem here is they started making all the ancillary stuff too so they threw everything out and now are not just stepping on what came before they are messing up their own new lore breaking the rules of the only canon they kept in place and also not replacing it with anything “better” just a mishmash of recycled ideas from previous stories and stolen ideas from the EU that are presented in new context without being earned. the acolyte has darth plaguis! oh really? did they do anything cool with him? no he just kinda lives in a cave like gollum and we see him for a second. he also isnt formally introduced so despite him making his live action debut the expectation is that we the fans know who he is already which is fair but they disposed of all of his cannon backstory outside of just what palpatine says in the prequel trilogy and his appearance here is neither in line with that or relevant to it. so a dismissal of the material an overwriting of it and a reliance of our knowledge of it to make their new broken version of it work. and that is just the one thing.

author: @WritingWrongsFilm
text:
We all love Star Wars and that love is what drives these debates forever. Given we saw the brand tarnished and defiled in ways we never thought possible. 

Did Kathleen contribute to the decline of Star Wars? Yes. 
Does she work under the authority of Bob Iger? Yes.
Just as Kevin Feige does....and we saw the same fate with Marvel. Which shows this is not a talent/creativity problem so much as a SYSTEMIC problem. Kennedy and Feige both were complicit and following orders. Even when every metric continued to drop with every failing project.

These choices were not creative in nature. They were propelled by agendas. When you understand just how REGULATED the creative process of these companies are. Its a testament to people like Favreau trying to make something like the first couple seasons of Mandalorian. But it too fell victim to the requirements. 

These creative restrictions force writers to write away from what is logical or even write away from canon. So we can point the finger at middle management. But they are just shills following orders and cashing a pay check. Iger, Kennedy, Feige - Do not make content for their CUSTOMERS....they make content for their CREDITORS.

author: @MLDPDarthJoker
text:
I can believe that Iger affected release schedules for films because with the run Disney had with the MCU in the 2010’s, Iger wanted more films ASAP & likely assumed Star Wars would have the same success with a high quantity of films like the MCU did in Phases 1-3.

“The Clone Wars” was originally a Cartoon Network show, so I can see why Iger would want it to end since CN is a Warner Bros-owned channel.

Development for “Obi-Wan Kenobi” & “Book Of Boba Fett” started before Disney+ launched, so I think those 2 films were reworked as shows as a way to market Disney+.

There hasn’t been a Star Wars film since 2019, so I think Iger wanted “The Mandalorian” season 4 reworked into a film since it might have the best chance of breaking even, which says a lot about Star Wars’ theatrical hopes given the low ratings for “The Mandalorian” season 3.

I think these were all business decisions, not creative decisions, cause that would require Iger to be creative. 

I don’t believe he cared enough about the story to shut down a lot of the creative decisions in Disney Star Wars without Kennedy giving her input as President of Lucasfilm. I think both of them agreed on not using George’s sequel outline & replacing legacy characters with Disney’s characters. I think Iger wanted it for business reasons while I think Kennedy wanted it for her ego.

I think Den Of Nerds is being given bad info, & it’s possible that his source is being given bad info.

Kennedy’s 72, so she’ll retire in a few years. I think all this is her using Iger’s fallen reputation to salvage her legacy. It won’t work. You don’t use your position as President of Lucasfilm to kill Star Wars & then expect to not get criticism.

Iger deserves plenty of blame since as CEO of Disney, he had the authority to reject many of Kennedy’s ideas & chose not to use that authority. Kennedy is President of Lucasfilm, so she deserves the most blame.

author: @ForceMaximus84
text:
Kathleen Kennedy is an eight-time-Oscar-nominated producer who has worked on some of the most successful and iconic movies of all time, including many made by two of the most powerful and influential men in Hollywood history. One of them being George Lucas, himself. Does anyone with a functioning brain really think she wouldn’t have the capacity to challenge Bob Iger on any decision made regarding Star Wars or Indiana Jones if she wanted to? Does anyone really think she wouldn’t have called out his BS after Last Jedi came out if it wasn’t her fault? No, this is a flat-out lie. She’s an accomplice and she can’t be trusted. Both need to be replaced by people who respects both Disney and Lucasfilm and the legacies of both men behind them. 

Lucas’ VII-IX or Nothing.

author: @TS_Ruin
text:
I think people need to let go of Star Wars. That time of the OT and PT are gone. It’s frustrating that the only thing keeping star wars ‘alive’ is the controversies and it’s quite sad. It’s in a vegetative state. Disney and the people running Star Wars are incompetent and you need to stop giving them the benefit of the doubt believing Star Wars will make a turn around someday. It’s not going to happen. I’m content with what I got with Star Wars growing up. 
I have the 1-6 films. The original games by lucasarts, and the legends material. 34 years old and I remember seeing the prequel movies in the theaters and having phantom menace on vhs. I remember my older brother sitting in the very front row in the theater and cheered when he saw Yoda come on screen to face Dooku in attack of the clones.  I remember my brother giving me KOTOR for Christmas for PC and we had my friends played the crap out of it. Experiencing Revan as a character and many memorable characters from that game. Bastila, Carth, mission, canderous, T3-M4, HK-47, Zaalbar, Juhani, and Jolee Bindo. Along with Republic commando, original battlefront, and remembering that Darth maul was so cheap and having hero battles on the mos eisley map. The legends material. Seeing how Luke really came to be so much more awesome in those stories. Mara Jade. The Thrawn trilogy, The Yuuzhan Vong War. And many others like Nomi sunrider and Darth bane and Exar kun. How return of the jedi is my favorite movie out of the OT. When Luke faced down Vader and refused Palpatine which is why it was so heart breaking to me to see what they turned Luke into in last Jedi. 

This isn’t the same Luke I’ve known from the movies or the legends. I knew it was a red flag when Disney came out and made the legends non canon. I knew exactly what they were going to do. They wanted to tell ‘their’ story. Their version of Star Wars. But I still gave it a chance. And after witnessing the sequel trilogy. What they really wanted. Is to have all the assets of Star Wars. The fans. The love the passion, The merchandising, the “legends material” they keep poaching from. And everything in between. But they didn’t want the responsibility of taking care of it. They thought they could just slap a Star Wars label whatever they dish out and people would just consume it. And for the most part they were successful. Why try harder with stories and characters if the name sells itself? In a most shallow way. And because our society has become so polarized by politics and beliefs. Any kind of criticism now is shrugged off as ists and isms and ics. Even though it makes no sense. It’s easier to deflect the criticism and label you as bigot to undermine your position than to accept accountability. We’re all inclusive unless you criticize our stuff and we will ostracize you. We don’t like the toxic fandom with its negativity, we want the toxic fandom with its positivity. 

Even with mountains of criticisms for the prequels and a fair share with the OT. I still love what George did. Things i absolutely disagree with now sure and would change but now I’m old enough to see that it can be soo mucch woorse. Despite the prequel criticisms. It expanded the lore and the material and gave us amazing content as I mentioned above. And it still felt like Star Wars. What did sequel trilogy expand on? The games are worse, with maybe a good game or 2 there compared to the plethora of great games back in the 2000s, who cares about the expanded books and material compared to the continued adventures of Luke Han and Leia? Disney tried to reinvent Star Wars without understanding it first. And as I’ve said, didn’t want the responsibility of taking care of it. They just reset the chessboard with different pieces and just tossed them back in the box and it looks all bloaty and wrong. 

I love Star Wars. Because it has directly affected my life. It gave me a love of space and technology, sci-fi  stories. With characters that are unforgettable with stories that I will remember the rest of my life.  But that doesn’t mean I need to continue to watch something I loved for so long be treated so badly. I held on for as long as I could and stopped after the rise of Skywalker. Disney can try to resuscitate Star Wars as long as they want but to no avail. How truer are the words of Han Solo in force awakens, “that’s not how the force works” and kylo Rens “let the past die, kill it if you have to.”  In last Jedi, and Rey’s “Rey Skywalker” in rise of skywalker. Because how the force works doesn’t matter. Holding on to the past doesn’t matter. And Luke Skywalker doesn’t matter. Because it’s Rey Skywalker. 

And none could have predicted that George signed away Star Wars’s death warrant back in 2012. Not even the visionary himself.

author: @hazardous-g2j
text:
Dave Filoni is not some amazing creative, clone wars was when George Lucas was there, Dave Filoni just carried it when Lucas left. Filoni has failed creatively with anything he has been responsible for if it requires any ability other then turning up.

The whole Disney and all the associated studios have/had so much money they pushed out all the creative people and concentrated on DEI and messaging. The money has allowed them to get to this point where they have zero ability to produce anything an audience would want to watch,

And KK started the rot before Iger decided he had to step in but had zero ability to make it any better because the whole machine was toxic by then and he never had any ability to make anything but accounting/business decisions, he is the CEO who put had an insanely bad team which was entirely his fault.
